title: pteridium aquilinum subsp. esculentum
intro: Pteridium aquilinum subsp. esculentum, commonly known as bracken, is a type of fern that grows in many parts of the world, particularly in temperate regions. It has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing the risk of certain cancers and improving cardiovascular health. Bracken is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
